name: reply
title: Discord Reply
description: Reply on the messaging channel. Pass chat_id from the inbound message. Optional: reply_to, files, embeds, components.

chat_id.description: Channel ID where the message will be sent

reply_to.description: Message ID to thread under. Use message_id from the inbound <channel> block, or an id from fetch.

files.description: Absolute file paths to attach (images, logs, etc). Max 10 files, 25MB each.

embeds.description: Discord embed objects. Fields: title, description, color (int), fields [{name, value, inline}], footer {text}, timestamp.

components.description: Discord message components. Use Action Rows containing Buttons, Select Menus, etc. See Discord Components V2 docs.

name: react
title: Reaction
description: Add an emoji reaction to a message. Unicode direct; custom emoji as <:name:id>.

message_id.description: ID of the message to react to

emoji.description: Unicode emoji (e.g. "👍") or custom emoji in <:name:id> format

name: edit_message
title: Edit Message
description: Edit a previously-sent bot message. Supports text, embeds, components.

message_id.description: ID of the bot message to edit

name: download_attachment
title: Download Attachment
description: Download attachments from a message to the local inbox. Returns file paths ready to Read.

message_id.description: ID of the message with attachments

name: fetch
title: Fetch
description: Fetch recent messages from a channel. Returns oldest-first with message IDs.

channel.description: Channel name label (e.g. "main", "general") as configured in channelsConfig

name: schedule_status
title: Schedule Status
description: Show configured schedules with next fire time and running state.

name: trigger_schedule
title: Trigger Schedule
description: Manually trigger a named schedule immediately, ignoring time/day constraints.

name: schedule_control
title: Schedule Control
description: Defer or skip a schedule. action: "defer" suppresses for N minutes (default 30) or "skip_today" for the rest of the day.

minutes.description: Defer duration in minutes (default 30, only for defer action)

name: activate_channel_bridge
title: Activate Channel Bridge
description: Activate or deactivate the channel bridge. Active: inbound messages trigger typing/reactions and auto-forward transcript to Discord. Inactive: only direct MCP tool calls work.

active.description: true to activate, false to deactivate

name: reload_config
title: Reload Config
description: Reload config from disk; re-register schedules, webhooks, event rules without restart.

name: memory
title: Memory Cycle
description: Run memory operations: cycle1, cycle2/sleep (promote+dedup), flush, prune, status, remember (store fact), forget (archive a root), backfill, rebuild, delete. Destructive ops (rebuild, prune, delete) require matching `confirm` string.

element.description: Content for remember; also accepted as a substring match target for forget

maxDays.description: Age threshold in days for the `prune` action. Unclassified entries older than this are deleted. Default 30, minimum 1. Ignored by other actions.

confirm.description: Required for destructive actions: "DELETE ALL MEMORY" for delete, "PRUNE OLD ENTRIES" for prune, "REBUILD MEMORY" for rebuild. Must match exactly.

name: recall
title: Recall
description: Search past project / session memory. `query`: single NL string for one synthesized answer, or array of strings for unrelated multi-question (parallel mechanical merge). Codebase → explore, web → search.

query.description: Single NL string, or array of strings for unrelated multi-question.

background.description: Default false (sync). Set true for heavy queries to dispatch async and receive answer via channel.

name: explore
title: Explore
description: Search internal codebase / filesystem. `query`: single NL string for one synthesized answer, or array of strings for unrelated multi-question. Omit `cwd` for the current workspace; set only when the user names a path. Past memory → recall, web → search.

query.description: Single NL string, or array of strings for unrelated multi-question.

cwd.description: Optional search root. Omit for current workspace; pass only when the user names a path.

background.description: Default false (sync). Set true for heavy queries to dispatch async and receive answer via channel.

name: search
title: Search
description: External web / URL scrape. `query`: single NL string for one synthesized answer, or array of strings for unrelated multi-question. URL → scrape. Past memory → recall, codebase → explore.

query.description: Single NL string, or array of strings for unrelated multi-question.

background.description: Default false (sync). Set true for heavy queries to dispatch async and receive answer via channel.

name: create_session
title: Create Session
description: Create external AI session with tool access. preset: full/readonly/mcp. agent: Worker/Reviewer.

provider.description: openai, anthropic, gemini, groq, openrouter, xai, copilot, ollama, lmstudio, local

model.description: Provider-specific model id (e.g. "gpt-5", "claude-opus-4", "gemini-2.5-pro"). Must be valid for the chosen provider.

systemPrompt.description: Optional system prompt prepended to the session. When omitted, the plugin injects the default Pool B/C prefix based on role/preset.

preset.description: Tool permission preset: `full` grants read+write+shell, `readonly` restricts to read-only tools, `mcp` exposes only MCP-bridged tools.

files.description: Optional virtual files seeded into the session's working context (path+content pairs). The agent can read these immediately without fs access.

name: list_sessions
title: List Sessions
description: List active orchestrator sessions.

name: close_session
title: Close Session
description: Close an orchestrator session.

sessionId.description: Session id to close (from create_session / list_sessions). Plants a tombstone, aborts in-flight work; re-closing is a no-op.

name: list_models
title: List Models
description: List available models from all providers.

name: bridge
title: Bridge to External Model
description: Delegate one turn to an external agent by role (mapped via user-workflow.json). Detached: returns jobId + sessionId; agent runs in background. Pass exactly one of prompt / ref / file. Use close_session to stop early. Bench escape hatch: pass provider+model directly (with optional effort/fast/tools/systemPrompt) to bypass agent-config.json — role still drives BP2 catalog scoping.

prompt.description: The task instruction for the agent.

role.description: Agent role from user-workflow.json (or any string for ad-hoc bench dispatch).

preset.description: Advanced: explicit preset name (bypass role mapping).

provider.description: Bench: provider id (e.g. anthropic-oauth, openai-oauth, gemini, deepseek, xai). Used together with model for ad-hoc dispatch — skips agent-config.json lookup.

model.description: Bench: model id matching the provider (e.g. claude-opus-4-7, gpt-5.5, gemini-3-flash-preview). Required when provider is set.

effort.description: Bench: reasoning effort (low / medium / high / xhigh). Used when provider+model are set.

fast.description: Bench: priority service tier flag (provider-specific). Used when provider+model are set.

tools.description: Bench: tool surface (full / readonly). Used when provider+model are set; default full.

systemPrompt.description: Bench: extra system text injected at the head of the prompt for ad-hoc dispatch.

context.description: Extra context appended to the prompt.

file.description: Read prompt from a file path.

cwd.description: Working directory for bridge agent tool execution.

name: read
title: Read
description: Read file(s). `path`: string or array for parallel multi-file. `mode`: full | head | tail | count. `n` sets head/tail lines; `offset`/`limit` set full-mode window. Files over byte cap require offset/limit, head, tail, count, or `grep`.

path.description: File path string, or array of paths for parallel multi-file read.

n.description: Lines for head / tail mode. Default 20.

full.description: Opt out of the big-file head/tail cap. Default false.

name: edit
title: Edit
description: Replace text in file(s). For 2+ edits use `edits` array (same file applies sequentially, different files in parallel). Single form is for one-off only. `replace_all:true` drops the uniqueness check.

old_string.description: Text to find (single-edit form, unique unless replace_all).

replace_all.description: Replace every occurrence instead of requiring unique match.

path.description: Per-edit path. Omit to reuse the top-level path.

edits.description: Array of edits. Each may specify its own `path`; otherwise reuses top-level `path`. Same-file edits sequential, cross-file parallel.

name: write
title: Write
description: Create or overwrite a file. Prefer `apply_patch` for multi-file or context-heavy edits. For 2+ whole-file writes pass `writes` array.

writes.description: Batch whole-file writes. Use when creating/replacing several files in one call.

name: bash
title: Bash
description: Execute a shell command. Batch related commands with `&&` or `;` in one call. `persistent:true` keeps cwd/env/venv across calls. `run_in_background:true` for long jobs, then `job_wait` + `read` stdout/stderr paths. Destructive patterns (rm -rf /, force-push, format) are blocked.

merge_stderr.description: Merge stderr into stdout (2>&1). Default false: stderr surfaced as separate `[stderr]` block.

run_in_background.description: Run command in the background and return a job id immediately. Use for long builds/tests/servers.

persistent.description: Keep shell state (cwd, env, venv, functions) across calls. One shared shell per session.

session_id.description: Explicit persistent shell session id to reuse. Prefer `persistent:true` unless targeting a specific shell.

name: job_wait
title: Wait For Background Job
description: Wait for a background shell job to finish and return its latest status/summary in one call.

job_id.description: Background job id returned by bash with run_in_background:true.

timeout_ms.description: Maximum time to wait before returning the current running state. Default 30000.

poll_ms.description: Polling interval while waiting. Default 250 ms.

name: grep
title: Grep
description: ripgrep content search. `pattern`: regex string (use `|` for alternation) or array (OR-joined). `glob`: same shape. For known identifier prefer `find_symbol`. Output modes: `files_with_matches` (default), `content`, `count`. `multiline:true` for cross-line patterns.

pattern.description: Regex string (use `|` for alternation) or array of patterns (OR-joined).

glob.description: Glob filter (`{a,b}` brace expansion supported) or array of filters.

-n.description: Show line numbers (content mode, default true).

name: glob
title: Glob
description: File path search via `rg --files`. `pattern`: glob string (`{a,b}` brace expansion supported) or array (OR-joined). Prefer array for genuinely independent categories. For in-file content search use `grep`.

pattern.description: Glob string (`{a,b}` brace expansion supported) or array of glob patterns.

path.description: Base dir. Default: cwd. Result rows emitted as absolute paths.

head_limit.description: Max file paths to return. Default 100; 0 = unlimited.

offset.description: Skip N file paths before applying head_limit.

name: list
title: List Directory
description: Directory inspection. `mode`: list (metadata: name/type/size/mtime) | tree (ASCII) | find (filter by name/size/mtime). For newest files use `list sort:"mtime"` or `find`. For repo-wide filename pattern use `glob`; for in-file content use `grep`; for symbols use `find_symbol`.

path.description: Root directory. Default cwd. Supports `~`. All result rows emitted as absolute paths.

depth.description: Recursion depth. list: 1 default, max 10. tree: 3 default, max 6.

sort.description: list mode sort key. Default name.

type.description: Filter by entry type. Default any.

offset.description: Skip N rows/entries before applying head_limit.

min_size.description: find mode: minimum size in bytes (file only).

max_size.description: find mode: maximum size in bytes (file only).

modified_after.description: find mode: ISO 8601 date or relative `Nh`/`Nd` (e.g. `24h`, `7d`).

modified_before.description: find mode: ISO 8601 date or relative `Nh`/`Nd`.

name: find_symbol
title: Find Symbol
description: Symbol-level navigation across the repository. Default mode finds the declaration site for a known identifier (file:line + nearby code). Pass `mode` to switch into graph queries: `callers` (call sites for functions), `references` (all references — use for non-function symbols too), `imports` (what a file imports), `dependents` (who imports a file), or `overview`/`symbols`/`related`/`impact` for file-level analysis. Prefer over grep when the identifier is known but the file is not.

symbol.description: Symbol or identifier. Required for default/callers/references modes; optional for impact.

mode.description: Query mode. Omit or use "symbol" for declaration lookup. "callers"/"references" for usage. "imports"/"dependents" for module graph. "overview"/"symbols"/"related"/"impact" for file-level analysis.

file.description: Target file path. Required for imports/dependents/related/impact/symbols. Optional for callers/references (narrows language).

language.description: Optional language filter (e.g. javascript, typescript, python).

name: apply_patch
title: Apply Unified Diff
description: Apply a unified-diff patch in ONE turn. Prefer over repeated read→edit loops for 2+ files or 2+ hunks. Git-style headers; `/dev/null` for create/delete. Default atomic (`reject_partial:true`); `dry_run:true` to preview.

patch.description: Unified diff. Single or multi-file. Git-style `a/`/`b/` headers; `/dev/null` for create/delete.

base_path.description: Directory to resolve diff-header paths against. Default cwd.

dry_run.description: Preview without writing. Shows first failed hunk per file. Default false.

reject_partial.description: If any hunk fails, reject the whole patch (atomic). Default true; false to apply each successful file independently.

name: inject_input
description: Inject text into host terminal (parent console). Always submits — appends newline if absent.

text.description: Text to type into the host terminal.
